Oracle Database Administrator (Cloud) – Job Description

The Oracle Database Administrator (Cloud) is responsible for managing, securing, and optimizing Oracle databases hosted in cloud and hybrid environments, with a strong focus on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI). The role ensures high availability, performance, scalability, security, and cost efficiency of cloud-based database platforms supporting mission-critical applications.

Key Responsibilities

Cloud Database Administration (OCI-Focused) - Provision, configure, and manage Oracle Databases on OCI: - Oracle Autonomous Database (ATP/ADW) - Database Cloud Service (DBCS) - Exadata Cloud Service / Exadata Cloud@Customer - Manage Single Instance, RAC, CDB/PDB architectures in cloud environments. - Perform database lifecycle management: provisioning, scaling, cloning, patching, and decommissioning.

Hybrid & Migration Responsibilities - Support hybrid environments (on-prem + OCI). - Plan and execute database migrations to the cloud using: - RMAN - Data Pump - OCI Database Migration Service - Goldengate (if applicable) - Validate performance and functionality post-migration. - Support cross-region and cross-tenancy architectures.

High Availability, DR & Resilience - Design and manage: - OCI Data Guard - Automated backups and recovery - Cross-region disaster recovery - Perform recovery testing and DR drills. - Ensure compliance with RTO/RPO objectives. - Manage rolling upgrades and patching with minimal downtime.

Performance & Cost Optimization - Monitor and tune databases using: - OCI Console - AWR / ADDM / ASH - OCI Monitoring & Alarms - Optimize: - Storage (block/object) - Compute shapes - Licensing and resource utilization - Implement cost-aware architectures and usage tracking (FinOps).

Security, Compliance & Governance - Implement cloud security best practices: - IAM policies - Network security (VCNs, NSGs, Security Lists) - Encryption (TDE, OCI Vault) - Manage database access controls, auditing, and separation of duties. - Support compliance requirements (ISO, SOX, GDPR, etc.). - Perform vulnerability remediation and security hardening.

Automation & DevOps Enablement - Automate database operations using: - Shell scripting - SQL / PL/SQL - OCI CLI & SDK - Terraform / Ansible - Integrate database changes into CI/CD pipelines. - Support Infrastructure as Code (IaC) for database provisioning. - Enable self-service patterns for development teams.

Monitoring, Incident & Operations Management - Set up proactive monitoring, alerts, and dashboards. - Troubleshoot cloud-specific issues (latency, connectivity, scaling). - Participate in 24x7 on-call rotations for production support. - Perform root cause analysis (RCA) and implement preventive controls.

Documentation & Cross-Team Collaboration - Maintain cloud architecture diagrams and operational runbooks. - Work closely with: - Cloud architects - DevOps engineers - Application teams - Security and compliance teams - Provide technical guidance and mentoring to junior DBAs.

Education & Certifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in computer science / IT or related field
  • Preferred certifications:
  • Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) Architect or DBA
  • Oracle Certified Professional (OCP)
  • Exadata / Performance / Security certifications

Soft Skills

  • Strong cloud troubleshooting mindset
  • Automation-first approach
  • Clear communication with technical and non-technical teams
  • Ability to work independently in fast-paced cloud environments
  • Ownership and accountability for production systems

Experience Levels
